questions.q: How many distinct tangent lines does the nodal cubic $y^2 = x^3 + x^2$ have at its singular point $(0,0)$?
questions.explain: The degree-$2$ part at the origin is $y^2 - x^2 = (y-x)(y+x)$, which factors into two distinct linear forms. So the tangent cone is two lines $y = \pm x$, exactly the node picture.

questions.q: At the cusp of $y^2 = x^3$, the degree-$2$ part of the defining polynomial is:
questions.explain: The lowest-order terms of $y^2 - x^3$ at the origin are just $y^2$, a single repeated factor. That single tangent direction with multiplicity $2$ is what distinguishes a cusp from a node.

questions.q: For the cuspidal parametrization $(x,y) = (t^2, t^3)$, evaluate $y$ at $t = 3$.
questions.explain: $y = t^3 = 3^3 = 27$. The cusp $y^2 = x^3$ is parametrized by $t \mapsto (t^2,t^3)$, with $t=0$ mapping to the singular point.

questions.q: Let $E : y^2 = x^3 + ax + b$ with $a,b\in\mathbb{Z}$ and discriminant $\Delta = -16(4a^3 + 27 b^2)$. The reduction $\tilde E_p$ is singular (bad reduction) iff:
questions.explain: A Weierstrass cubic is smooth over $\mathbb{F}_p$ exactly when its discriminant is nonzero mod $p$. So bad reduction at $p$ is the condition $p \mid \Delta$; good reduction is $p \nmid \Delta$.
$\Delta = -16(4\cdot 0 + 27\cdot 1) = -16\cdot 27 = -432 = -2^4\cdot 3^3$, so the only primes of bad reduction for this model are $2$ and $3$.

questions.q: For $E : y^2 = x^3 + 1$ with $\Delta = -432 = -2^4\cdot 3^3$, at which prime below is the reduction good?
questions.explain: Bad primes are those dividing $\Delta = -432$, namely $p = 2$ and $p = 3$. Every other prime, including $p = 5$, is a prime of good reduction.

questions.q: In the Kodaira–Néron preview, the smooth locus of a cuspidal reduction $\tilde E_p$ is isomorphic to:
questions.explain: Cuspidal reduction is called additive precisely because the smooth locus is the additive group $\mathbb{G}_a(\mathbb{F}_p) = \mathbb{F}_p$, which has order $p$.

questions.q: A node over $\mathbb{F}_p$ is called split multiplicative when:
choices: the two tangent slopes are defined over $\mathbb{F}_p$
the two tangent slopes are conjugate over $\mathbb{F}_{p^2}$ but not in $\mathbb{F}_p$
questions.explain: Split means the tangent slopes $\pm s$ lie in $\mathbb{F}_p$, so the smooth locus is $\mathbb{G}_m(\mathbb{F}_p) \cong \mathbb{F}_p^\times$ of order $p-1$. Non-split means the slopes live in $\mathbb{F}_{p^2} \setminus \mathbb{F}_p$, giving a non-split torus of order $p+1$.

questions.q: For a non-split multiplicative reduction at $p = 7$, the smooth part of $\tilde E_p$ has order $p+1$. Compute this order.
questions.explain: The non-split torus has order $p + 1 = 7 + 1 = 8$. Split multiplicative would instead give $p - 1 = 6$; additive would give $p = 7$.
